0.text: Complete reference for the dgov command-line interface.
0.title: CLI Reference

1.html:
CommandDescription
dgov runExecute a plan file with quality gates
dgov statusShow current task state
dgov cleanupRemove zombie worktrees and tasks
dgov initBootstrap project configuration
dgov ledgerRecord bugs, rules, and technical debt
1.text: CommandDescriptiondgov runExecute a plan file with quality gatesdgov statusShow current task statedgov cleanupRemove zombie worktrees and tasksdgov initBootstrap project configurationdgov ledgerRecord bugs, rules, and technical debt
1.title: Commands Overview

2.html:
Execute a plan file (TOML) with isolated worktrees and Sentrux quality gates.

dgov run plan.toml
dgov run plan.toml --restart
dgov run plan.toml --continue
dgov run plan.toml --only=add-feature
2.text:
Execute a plan file (TOML) with isolated worktrees and Sentrux quality gates.
dgov run plan.toml
dgov run plan.toml --restart
dgov run plan.toml --continue
dgov run plan.toml --only=add-feature
2.title: dgov run

3.html:
FlagDescription
--restartRestart the plan from the beginning, clearing prior state
--continueContinue from where you left off, retrying failed tasks
--only <task>Run only the specified task and its dependencies
3.text: FlagDescription--restartRestart the plan from the beginning, clearing prior state--continueContinue from where you left off, retrying failed tasks--only <task>Run only the specified task and its dependencies
3.title: Flags

4.html:
Restart a plan from scratch: 
dgov run feature-plan.toml --restart
Retry failed tasks: 
dgov run feature-plan.toml --continue
Run a single task: 
dgov run feature-plan.toml --only=add-tests
4.text:
Restart a plan from scratch:
dgov run feature-plan.toml --restart
Retry failed tasks:
dgov run feature-plan.toml --continue
Run a single task:
dgov run feature-plan.toml --only=add-tests
4.title: Examples

5.html:
The command outputs task progress to stderr and final results to stdout. In JSON mode (--json), it outputs structured data:

{
  "status": "complete",
  "succeeded": 3,
  "failed": 0,
  "sentrux": {
    "degradation": false,
    "quality_before": 85,
    "quality_after": 87
  },
  "duration_s": 45.23
}
5.title: Output

6.html:
Show the current governor state — what's running, what's idle, and task counts.

dgov status
6.text:
Show the current governor state — what's running, what's idle, and task counts.
dgov status
6.title: dgov status

7.html:
Default output: 
$ dgov status
status: active
tasks: 5
active: 2
task_list:
  - slug: add-feature, state: active
  - slug: add-tests, state: pending
JSON output: 
$ dgov status --json
{
  "status": "active",
  "tasks": 5,
  "active": 2,
  "task_list": [
    {"slug": "add-feature", "state": "active"},
    {"slug": "add-tests", "state": "pending"}
  ]
}
7.text:
Default output:
$ dgov status
status: active
tasks: 5
active: 2
task_list:
  - slug: add-feature, state: active
  - slug: add-tests, state: pending
JSON output:
$ dgov status --json
{
  "status": "active",
  "tasks": 5,
  "active": 2,
  "task_list": [
    {"slug": "add-feature", "state": "active"},
    {"slug": "add-tests", "state": "pending"}
  ]
}
7.title: Examples

8.html:
Annihilate zombie worktrees — marks ACTIVE tasks as ABANDONED and removes worktrees.

Use this when:

  • A task crashed without proper cleanup
  • You killed a run with Ctrl+C and need to clean up
  • Worktrees are left in a broken state
dgov cleanup
8.text:
Annihilate zombie worktrees — marks ACTIVE tasks as ABANDONED and removes worktrees.
Use this when:

A task crashed without proper cleanup
You killed a run with Ctrl+C and need to clean up
Worktrees are left in a broken state

dgov cleanup
8.title: dgov cleanup

9.html:
1. Finds all tasks in ACTIVE state
  2. Removes their git worktrees
  3. Marks them as ABANDONED in the persistence layer
9.text:
Finds all tasks in ACTIVE state
Removes their git worktrees
Marks them as ABANDONED in the persistence layer
9.title: Behavior

10.html:
$ dgov cleanup
Cleaning up 3 active tasks...
  [removed worktree] add-feature
  [removed worktree] add-tests
  [skip worktree] fix-bug: worktree not found
Transitions complete: 2 tasks marked as ABANDONED.
10.text:
$ dgov cleanup
Cleaning up 3 active tasks...
  [removed worktree] add-feature
  [removed worktree] add-tests
  [skip worktree] fix-bug: worktree not found
Transitions complete: 2 tasks marked as ABANDONED.
10.title: Examples

11.html:
Bootstrap .dgov/project.toml for the current repository. Auto-detects language, source directory, and test directory.

dgov init
dgov init --force
11.text:
Bootstrap .dgov/project.toml for the current repository. Auto-detects language, source directory, and test directory.
dgov init
dgov init --force
11.title: dgov init

13.html:
The init command detects:

  • Language: Python, JavaScript, Rust, or Go (by file prevalence)
  • Source directory: src/, lib/, or current directory
  • Test directory: tests/ or test/
  • Tooling: Appropriate lint, format, and test commands
13.text:
The init command detects:

Language: Python, JavaScript, Rust, or Go (by file prevalence)
Source directory: src/, lib/, or current directory
Test directory: tests/ or test/
Tooling: Appropriate lint, format, and test commands
13.title: Auto-Detection

14.html:
Create new project config: 
$ dgov init
Created .dgov/project.toml
  language: python
  src_dir:  src/
  test_dir: tests/
Regenerate (overwrite): 
$ dgov init --force
Created .dgov/project.toml
  language: python
  src_dir:  src/
  test_dir: tests/
14.text:

14.title: Examples

15.html: Operational ledger — record and list bugs, rules, and technical debt.
15.text: Operational ledger — record and list bugs, rules, and technical debt.
15.title: dgov ledger

16.html:
SubcommandDescription
addAdd an entry to the ledger
listList ledger entries
resolveMark a ledger entry as resolved
16.text: SubcommandDescriptionaddAdd an entry to the ledgerlistList ledger entriesresolveMark a ledger entry as resolved
16.title: Subcommands

17.html:
dgov ledger add <category> <content>
Categories:
  • bug — Known bugs or issues to address
  • rule — Project conventions or rules
  • debt — Technical debt to pay down
Examples: 
# Record a known bug
dgov ledger add bug "Race condition in task_runner.py when handling timeouts"
 
# Record a project rule
dgov ledger add rule "Always use absolute imports, ruff-enforced"
 
# Record technical debt
dgov ledger add debt "Refactor the persistence layer to use async I/O"
17.title: dgov ledger add

18.html:
dgov ledger list [options]
Options:
OptionDescription
-c, --categoryFilter by category (bug, rule, debt)
-s, --statusFilter by status: open (default) or resolved
-q, --querySearch content by keyword
-r, --rootProject root directory
Examples: 
# List all open entries
dgov ledger list
 
# List only bugs
dgov ledger list --category=bug
 
# Search for specific content
dgov ledger list --query="race condition"
 
# List resolved rules
dgov ledger list --category=rule --status=resolved
18.text:
dgov ledger list [options]
Options:
OptionDescription-c, --categoryFilter by category (bug, rule, debt)-s, --statusFilter by status: open (default) or resolved-q, --querySearch content by keyword-r, --rootProject root directory
Examples:
# List all open entries
dgov ledger list
 
# List only bugs
dgov ledger list --category=bug
 
# Search for specific content
dgov ledger list --query="race condition"
 
# List resolved rules
dgov ledger list --category=rule --status=resolved
18.title: dgov ledger list

19.html:
dgov ledger resolve <entry_id>
Example: 
# Resolve entry #3
dgov ledger resolve 3
# Output: Resolved entry #3
19.text:
dgov ledger resolve <entry_id>
Example:
# Resolve entry #3
dgov ledger resolve 3
# Output: Resolved entry #3
19.title: dgov ledger resolve

20.html:
OptionDescription
--jsonOutput as JSON instead of human-readable text
--versionShow version and exit
--helpShow help message for any command
20.text: OptionDescription--jsonOutput as JSON instead of human-readable text--versionShow version and exit--helpShow help message for any command
20.title: Global Options

21.html:
Most commands support --json for machine-readable output:

dgov --json status
dgov run plan.toml --json
dgov ledger list --json

You can also enable JSON mode via environment variable:

 
dgov status  # Will output JSON
21.text:

21.title: JSON Mode

22.html: dgov is built on a set of core engineering principles designed to make multi-agent orchestration as predictable as a compiler.
22.text: dgov is built on a set of core engineering principles designed to make multi-agent orchestration as predictable as a compiler.
22.title: Concepts

23.html:
ModuleRole
kernel.pyPure (state, event) → (new_state, actions) — no I/O
runner.pyAsync DAG executor feeding the kernel
worker.pyStandalone OpenAI-client subprocess
settlement.pyruff auto-fix + lint gate + sentrux policy gate
worktree.pyGit worktree create/merge/remove
plan.pyTOML plan parsing, DAG compilation
persistence/SQLite event store (panes + events + slug history)
23.text: ModuleRolekernel.pyPure (state, event) → (new_state, actions) — no I/Orunner.pyAsync DAG executor feeding the kernelworker.pyStandalone OpenAI-client subprocesssettlement.pyruff auto-fix + lint gate + sentrux policy gateworktree.pyGit worktree create/merge/removeplan.pyTOML plan parsing, DAG compilationpersistence/SQLite event store (panes + events + slug history)
23.title: Architecture

24.html:
The heart of dgov is a pure state machine. It doesn't know about the network, the filesystem, or LLMs. It only knows how to transition state based on events (like TaskDone or TaskFailed) and emit actions (like MergeTask or RetryTask).

This separation makes the orchestration logic perfectly testable and resilient to process crashes.
24.text:
The heart of dgov is a pure state machine. It doesn't know about the network, the filesystem, or LLMs. It only knows how to transition state based on events (like TaskDone or TaskFailed) and emit actions (like MergeTask or RetryTask).
This separation makes the orchestration logic perfectly testable and resilient to process crashes.
24.title: Deterministic Kernel

25.html:
Every task dispatched by dgov runs in its own git worktree.

  • Isolated Environment: Workers cannot accidentally interfere with each other's files.
  • Commit-or-Kill: Changes are only merged back to the main branch if they pass all settlement gates (linting, tests, and architectural checks).
  • Audit Trail: Every attempt is a branch. You can always inspect exactly what a worker was doing by checking its worktree.
25.text:
Every task dispatched by dgov runs in its own git worktree.

Isolated Environment: Workers cannot accidentally interfere with each other's files.
Commit-or-Kill: Changes are only merged back to the main branch if they pass all settlement gates (linting, tests, and architectural checks).
Audit Trail: Every attempt is a branch. You can always inspect exactly what a worker was doing by checking its worktree.
25.title: Snapshot Isolation

26.html:
The settlement layer acts as a automated PR review. Before any code is merged, dgov performs:

  1. Autofix: Running formatters and simple lint fixes.
  2. Review: Fast sanity checks (e.g. no secrets, no binary files).
  3. Validation: Running the project's own test suite and architectural gates.
26.text:
The settlement layer acts as a automated PR review. Before any code is merged, dgov performs:

Autofix: Running formatters and simple lint fixes.
Review: Fast sanity checks (e.g. no secrets, no binary files).
Validation: Running the project's own test suite and architectural gates.
26.title: Settlement Layer

27.html: Deterministic kernel for multi-agent orchestration via git worktrees.
27.text: Deterministic kernel for multi-agent orchestration via git worktrees.
27.title: Getting Started

28.html:
uv pip install -e .

Requires Python 3.12+, git.
28.text:
uv pip install -e .
Requires Python 3.12+, git.
28.title: Install

29.html:
dgov init                # Bootstrap .dgov/project.toml
dgov run plan.toml       # Execute a plan
dgov status              # Show task status
dgov watch               # Stream events
dgov sentrux check       # Run architectural quality check
dgov cleanup             # Annihilate zombie worktrees
29.text:
dgov init                # Bootstrap .dgov/project.toml
dgov run plan.toml       # Execute a plan
dgov status              # Show task status
dgov watch               # Stream events
dgov sentrux check       # Run architectural quality check
dgov cleanup             # Annihilate zombie worktrees
29.title: Usage

30.html:
Plans are TOML files that compile to DAGs:

[plan]
name = "example"
 
[[unit]]
slug = "add-feature"
prompt = "Add the feature to src/foo.py"
agent = "kimi-k2.5"
files = ["src/foo.py"]
 
[[unit]]
slug = "add-tests"
prompt = "Write tests for the feature"
agent = "kimi-k2.5"
files = ["tests/test_foo.py"]
depends_on = ["add-feature"]
30.title: Plan format

31.html: A plan.toml file declares a deterministic execution graph. It is the contract between the Governor and Workers.
31.text: A plan.toml file declares a deterministic execution graph. It is the contract between the Governor and Workers.
31.title: Plan Reference

32.html:
[plan]
name = "refactor-api"
default_agent = "claude-3-5-sonnet"
max_retries = 2
 
[tasks.validate]
summary = "Validate current state"
prompt = "Review src/api.py and verify the current endpoint structure"
agent = "reviewer"
commit_message = "docs: add validation notes"
 
[tasks.validate.files]
read = ["src/api.py"]
 
[tasks.migrate]
summary = "Migrate to new schema"
prompt = "Refactor src/api.py to use the new typed response schema"
depends_on = ["validate"]
commit_message = "refactor: migrate api to typed schema"
 
[tasks.migrate.files]
edit = ["src/api.py"]
create = ["src/api/types.py"]
32.title: Structure Overview

33.html:
The plan metadata and global defaults.

FieldTypeRequiredDefaultDescription
namestringYes—Unique identifier for this plan
default_agentstringNo—Fallback agent for tasks without explicit agent
max_retriesintegerNo3Global retry limit for failed tasks
project_rootstringNo.Path to project root relative to plan file
session_rootstringNo.Path to session work directory
33.text:
The plan metadata and global defaults.
FieldTypeRequiredDefaultDescriptionnamestringYes—Unique identifier for this plandefault_agentstringNo—Fallback agent for tasks without explicit agentmax_retriesintegerNo3Global retry limit for failed tasksproject_rootstringNo.Path to project root relative to plan filesession_rootstringNo.Path to session work directory
33.title: [plan] Section

34.html:
When a task runs, its agent is resolved in this priority:

  1. tasks.<slug>.agent
  2. plan.default_agent
  3. project.toml default

If none are set, validation fails before execution.
34.text:
When a task runs, its agent is resolved in this priority:

tasks.<slug>.agent
plan.default_agent
project.toml default

If none are set, validation fails before execution.
34.title: Agent Resolution Order

35.html:
Individual task definitions. Use [tasks.slug] for inline tables or [[tasks]] array-of-tables syntax.

FieldTypeRequiredDefaultDescription
slugstringYes (implicit)—Task identifier (from table key)
summarystringYes—Short description for logs and UI
promptstringYes—Instructions sent to the worker agent
agentstringNoplan.default_agentAgent model for this task
depends_onarray of stringsNo[]Slugs that must complete first
commit_messagestringYes—Git commit message on success
timeout_sintegerNo900Seconds before task aborts
35.text:
Individual task definitions. Use [tasks.slug] for inline tables or [[tasks]] array-of-tables syntax.
FieldTypeRequiredDefaultDescriptionslugstringYes (implicit)—Task identifier (from table key)summarystringYes—Short description for logs and UIpromptstringYes—Instructions sent to the worker agentagentstringNoplan.default_agentAgent model for this taskdepends_onarray of stringsNo[]Slugs that must complete firstcommit_messagestringYes—Git commit message on successtimeout_sintegerNo900Seconds before task aborts
35.title: [tasks.<slug>] Section

36.html:
File claims enforce isolation between concurrent tasks. The kernel validates that independent tasks (no dependency edge) never claim overlapping file paths.

FieldTypeDescription
createarray of stringsFiles the task will create
editarray of stringsFiles the task will modify
deletearray of stringsFiles the task will remove
readarray of stringsFiles the task reads (informational, not enforced)
36.text:
File claims enforce isolation between concurrent tasks. The kernel validates that independent tasks (no dependency edge) never claim overlapping file paths.
FieldTypeDescriptioncreatearray of stringsFiles the task will createeditarray of stringsFiles the task will modifydeletearray of stringsFiles the task will removereadarray of stringsFiles the task reads (informational, not enforced)
36.title: File Claims [tasks.<slug>.files]

37.html:
File claims are the foundation of deterministic concurrency:

  1. Declaration: Every task must declare files it touches
  2. Validation: Before execution, the kernel checks for conflicts
  3. Enforcement: Independent tasks with overlapping paths fail validation
37.text:
File claims are the foundation of deterministic concurrency:

Declaration: Every task must declare files it touches
Validation: Before execution, the kernel checks for conflicts
Enforcement: Independent tasks with overlapping paths fail validation
37.title: File Claim Isolation

38.html:
[tasks.a]
summary = "Update config"
prompt = "Change port to 8080"
files.edit = ["config.yaml"]
 
[tasks.b]
summary = "Also update config"
prompt = "Change port to 9090"
files.edit = ["config.yaml"]  # ERROR: no dependency edge with task.a
38.title: Conflict Example (Invalid)

39.html:
[tasks.a]
summary = "Update config"
files.edit = ["config.yaml"]
 
[tasks.b]
summary = "Read updated config"
files.read = ["config.yaml"]
depends_on = ["a"]  # OK: explicit ordering prevents conflict
39.text:
[tasks.a]
summary = "Update config"
files.edit = ["config.yaml"]
 
[tasks.b]
summary = "Read updated config"
files.read = ["config.yaml"]
depends_on = ["a"]  # OK: explicit ordering prevents conflict
39.title: Valid Dependency Chain

40.html:
Conflicts are detected when paths are identical or one is a parent of another:

  • src/main.py overlaps with src/main.py
  • src/ overlaps with src/main.py
  • config.yaml does NOT overlap with other.yaml
40.text:
Conflicts are detected when paths are identical or one is a parent of another:

src/main.py overlaps with src/main.py
src/ overlaps with src/main.py
config.yaml does NOT overlap with other.yaml
40.title: Path Overlap Rules

41.html:
[plan]
name = "payment-gateway-refactor"
default_agent = "claude-3-opus"
max_retries = 2
project_root = "."
 
# === Phase 1: Analysis ===
 
[tasks.audit]
summary = "Audit current payment code"
prompt = """
Review src/payments/ directory and identify:
1. All external API calls
2. Error handling gaps
3. Missing type annotations
 
Write findings to audit_report.md
"""
agent = "senior-reviewer"
commit_message = "docs: payment system audit report"
timeout_s = 1200
 
[tasks.audit.files]
read = [
    "src/payments/",
    "src/payments/stripe.py",
    "src/payments/paypal.py",
]
create = ["audit_report.md"]
 
# === Phase 2: Design ===
 
[tasks.design_schema]
summary = "Design new payment schema"
prompt = "Based on audit_report.md, design typed models for PaymentMethod and PaymentResult"
depends_on = ["audit"]
commit_message = "design: payment type schema"
 
[tasks.design_schema.files]
read = ["audit_report.md"]
create = ["src/payments/types.py"]
 
# === Phase 3: Implementation ===
 
[tasks.refactor_stripe]
summary = "Migrate Stripe integration"
prompt = "Refactor src/payments/stripe.py to use new types from src/payments/types.py"
depends_on = ["design_schema"]
commit_message = "refactor: migrate stripe to typed models"
 
[tasks.refactor_stripe.files]
edit = ["src/payments/stripe.py"]
read = ["src/payments/types.py"]
 
[tasks.refactor_paypal]
summary = "Migrate PayPal integration"
prompt = "Refactor src/payments/paypal.py to use new types from src/payments/types.py"
depends_on = ["design_schema"]
commit_message = "refactor: migrate paypal to typed models"
 
[tasks.refactor_paypal.files]
edit = ["src/payments/paypal.py"]
read = ["src/payments/types.py"]
 
# === Phase 4: Cleanup ===
 
[tasks.remove_legacy]
summary = "Remove legacy payment module"
prompt = "Delete src/payments/legacy.py after confirming no imports remain"
depends_on = ["refactor_stripe", "refactor_paypal"]
commit_message = "chore: remove legacy payment module"
 
[tasks.remove_legacy.files]
read = ["src/payments/stripe.py", "src/payments/paypal.py"]
delete = ["src/payments/legacy.py"]
41.title: Full Example

42.html:
Validate a plan without executing:

dgov validate plan.toml

This checks:

  • TOML syntax and schema
  • All dependencies reference existing tasks
  • No file conflicts between independent tasks
  • All tasks have resolvable agents
42.text:
Validate a plan without executing:
dgov validate plan.toml
This checks:

TOML syntax and schema
All dependencies reference existing tasks
No file conflicts between independent tasks
All tasks have resolvable agents
42.title: Validation
